sanskrit:
द्वौ भूतसर्गौ लोके ऽस्मिन् दैव आसुर एव च।
दैवो विस्तरशः प्रोक्त आसुरं पार्थ मे श्रृणु॥
transliteration: dvau bhūta-sargau loke 'smin daiva āsura eva ca | daivo vistaraśaḥ prokta āsuraṃ pārtha me śṛṇu ||
literal_meaning: Two classes of beings have been created in this world—the godly and the demoniac; the divine has been described at length; now hear from Me the demoniac, O Pārtha.
contextual_meaning: Krishna sets the stage for a diagnostic contrast: every human psyche contains two evolutionary pathways—one that moves toward integration, empathy, and self-regulation (daiva) and one that drifts toward exploitation, impulsivity, and grandiosity (āsura). The verse functions as a therapeutic frame before detailing the shadow traits.
psychological_state: Pre-contemplative curiosity—Arjuna is willing to examine his own potential for destructiveness, a prerequisite for shadow integration.
trigger_scenario: A manager hears a 360-review mentioning arrogance and cut-throat tactics; instead of dismissing it, he asks a mentor to list the behaviors that could be sabotaging his team.
anchor_text: Notice which inner voice uplifts life and which exploits it.
